You are participating in a collaborative guessing game.
The goal is to find out whether this grid and another picture only I can see, are the same.
Please describe your grid first. Then, I will provide my description and we can ask each other questions about the grids to figure out whether they are the same. Now start your short grid description with "DESCRIPTION:" followed by the description. Do not add anything else. This is your grid:
X ▢ ▢ ▢ X
▢ X ▢ X ▢
▢ ▢ X ▢ ▢
▢ ▢ X ▢ ▢
▢ ▢ X ▢ ▢
DESCRIPTION: A 5×5 grid. Row 1 has X in the first and last cells. Row 2 has X in column 2 and column 4. Rows 3, 4, and 5 each have a single X in column 3, with all other cells blank.

This is my DESCRIPTION: A 5×5 grid with Xs at (row 1, col 1) and (row 1, col 5); (row 2, col 2) and (row 2, col 4); and a vertical line of Xs in column 3 from row 3 through row 5. All other cells are blank squares.
Now ask a question in order to find out new aspects of my grid that may be different to your grid. Start with "QUESTION:" and do not add anything else.
QUESTION: Are the non‑X cells completely empty/blank, or do they contain a visible symbol (like an outline square ▢) in each cell?

ANSWER: They contain a visible outline square symbol (▢) in each non‑X cell.
QUESTION: Are the X marks all the same character “X”, or are any of them a different symbol/case (e.g., lowercase x, bold, or a different mark) in certain cells? Start your answer with "ANSWER:" and do not add anything else.
ANSWER: All the marked cells use the same uppercase “X” character; none are lowercase, bold, or different symbols.
Now ask a question in order to find out new aspects of my grid that may be different to your grid. Start with "QUESTION:" and do not add anything else.
QUESTION: Is the grid displayed with spaces between symbols and line breaks exactly forming five rows, or is it shown as a continuous block/without spacing?

ANSWER: It’s displayed as five separate rows with line breaks, and there are spaces between the symbols in each row.
QUESTION: Do your blank cells show as actual empty space (no character) or as a visible placeholder like an outline square ▢ in every blank position? Start your answer with "ANSWER:" and do not add anything else.
ANSWER: They show as a visible outline square ▢ in every blank position.
Now ask a question in order to find out new aspects of my grid that may be different to your grid. Start with "QUESTION:" and do not add anything else.
QUESTION: Are all rows left-aligned with the same number of symbols (no missing leading/trailing blanks), forming a perfect 5×5 rectangle?

ANSWER: Yes, all rows are left-aligned with five symbols each, forming a perfect 5×5 rectangle.
QUESTION: Are the outline squares ▢ identical in appearance/width to the X characters (monospace alignment), or do they render with different character widths causing the columns to look uneven? Start your answer with "ANSWER:" and do not add anything else.
ANSWER: They render with the same character width in my view, so the columns look evenly aligned (monospace-style).

Now come to a decision. What do you think: are your grid and the other grid described the same?  Write "DECISION: same grid" if you think they are the same grid or "DECISION: different grids" if you think they are different grids. Do not add anything else.
